mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 134,580k suggests ongoing recovery momentum
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation measured by CPIAUCSL shows no acceleration above 2.5% annually, indicating sustained monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through low discount rates and positive real yields expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and CPIAUCSL at 211.49 indicate accommodative stance
regime_hint: zero-rate monetary accommodation with price stability
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods meeting trigger conditions, or correlation between (FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL) joint signal and forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling weakening residential investment and forward-looking consumer confidence deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550k units suggests moderate construction activity
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances where HOUST drops > 10% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production measured by INDPRO exhibits a 3-month moving average decline of more than 1.5%, indicating contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness that compresses profit margins and drives negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.5966 shows current production levels
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of periods with INDPRO 3-month MA decline > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales measured by RSAFS and real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 both show quarter-over-quarter acceleration above 2.5%, indicating broad-based demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 1.5% AND GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344,649M and GDPC1 at 11,599.4B indicate solid demand
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumption and investment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods meeting both trigger conditions, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate measured by UNRATE rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and forward-looking recession risk that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% reflects ongoing labor market recovery
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains below 0.50% while payroll employment measured by PAYEMS grows at least 100,000 persons monthly, indicating monetary accommodation supporting labor market strength without inflation pressure, producing positive 5-day equity returns as the Fed maintains supportive policy.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and PAYEMS at 134,580k support accommodative narrative
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with labor market recovery
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in more than 50% of periods meeting both conditions, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and retail sales measured by RSAFS both decline by more than 5% month-over-month simultaneously, indicating synchronized contraction in consumer and residential demand that signals recession risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% MoM AND RSAFS declines > 5% MoM in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 550k and RSAFS at 344,649M provide baseline for decline measurement
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods where both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
